notes: Lava is introduced as a new environmental fluid. In v0.1 it is LOCATION-BOUND — the Magma Tap 5 m must be placed directly over a lava pool and consumes the lava in-situ; the game's fluid pipes do not route lava. The record is nevertheless shaped as a full `fluid` record (mirroring base-game Water) so that pumpability can be enabled in a future revision without migrating data or adding a new resource kind. Mass/volume/stack properties are null for now — same stance as base-game Water, which has not published infobox values either. `producedBy` is empty (lava is environmental, not crafted); `consumedBy` lists only the Magma Tap; `storedIn` is empty (no lava tank block exists or is planned). Biome placement is first-pass, pending official volcanic-biome specification.

resources.description: Molten silicate rock occurring naturally on volcanic worlds. In v0.1 lava is location-bound: the Magma Tap 5 m must be sited directly over a lava pool and consumes the lava in-place at extremely high enthalpy. Lava is NOT routable through conveyors or fluid pipes in v0.1 — the record is kept as a fluid so a future revision can enable pumpability (via a dedicated Lava Pipe block, say) without a data migration. Direct touch contact is lethal to characters and cosmetically destructive to most blocks; only blocks rated for lava contact (currently only the Magma Tap) survive prolonged exposure.
